2605.23332.title: Cultural Adaptation in Large Language Models for Political Discourse
2605.23332.paper_summary: The integration of large language models into political discourse analysis creates new opportunities for comparative research, policy analysis, and civic technology, while introducing material risks for democratic accountability. This paper argues that cultural adaptation is a prerequisite for trustworthy deployment of large language models in political communication across diverse linguistic and institutional contexts. Current systems remain shaped by English dominant data, uneven multilingual coverage, and assumptions grounded in a narrow range of political institutions and discourse conventions, producing systematic errors when applied across cultures. We formalize cultural adaptation across translation, discourse, and ontology levels, identify recurring cultural failure modes in political NLP, and propose an operational evaluation matrix grounded in cultural fidelity, calibration, and democratic safety. Building on political text analysis, sociotechnical auditing, and cross cultural pragmatics, we outline methodological pathways including participatory dataset development, culturally aware transfer learning, and benchmark design that makes cultural adaptation empirically measurable. We conclude by clarifying governance constraints and scope conditions under which culturally adaptive political NLP can support democratic legitimacy.
2605.23332.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型在政治話語分析中的整合為比較研究、政策分析和公民技術創造了新的機會，同時也引入了對民主問責制的實質風險。本文主張，文化適應是大型語言模型在多樣語言和制度背景下進行政治交流可信部署的前提。當前系統仍然受到以英語為主的數據、語言多樣性覆蓋不均和基於狹窄範圍的政治機構及話語慣例的假設所影響，這在跨文化應用時會產生系統性錯誤。我們在翻譯、話語和本體層面上形式化文化適應，識別政治自然語言處理中的重複文化失敗模式，並提出一個基於文化忠實性、校準和民主安全的運營評估矩陣。在政治文本分析、社會技術審計和跨文化語用學的基礎上，我們概述了方法論路徑，包括參與式數據集開發、文化意識的遷移學習和使文化適應可實證測量的基準設計。我們最後澄清了治理約束和文化適應性政治自然語言處理可以支持民主合法性的範圍條件。
2605.23332.author: Wajdi Zaghouani et.al.
